findings.summary: finalize_document_with_error does not catch PAPERLESS_CALL_EXCEPTIONS around update calls, so network failure during error finalization propagates up unlike peer helpers in the same module.
evidence: tags.py: client.update_document() and client.update_document_metadata() called without try/except, unlike release_processing_tag and remove_stale_queue_tag in the same file
findings.suggestion: Wrap the update calls in finalize_document_with_error with try/except PAPERLESS_CALL_EXCEPTIONS consistent with peer functions.

findings.summary: PaperlessClient.update_document_metadata silently ignores unrecognized field names, so typos cause silent data loss.
evidence: paperless.py: 'if value is None or key not in self._METADATA_FIELDS: continue' silently drops unknown keys
findings.suggestion: Log a warning when an unrecognized key is passed to update_document_metadata.

findings.summary: ClassificationProcessor uses the initial tags snapshot for error finalization while OcrProcessor refreshes tags before finalization, creating inconsistent staleness behavior.
evidence: classifier/worker.py passes current_tags from process start to _finalize_with_error
findings.suggestion: Have the classifier refresh tags before error finalization to match the OCR worker pattern.

findings.summary: PaperlessClient methods call both _raise_for_status_if_server_error inside _get/_patch and response.raise_for_status() in callers, creating an undocumented two-tier error checking pattern.
evidence: get_document and download_content call response.raise_for_status() again after _get returns
findings.suggestion: Document the two-tier pattern or have _get/_patch/_post call raise_for_status() unconditionally after retries.

findings.summary: Tag IDs like 443, 552, 555 are hardcoded magic numbers in test files rather than referencing factory defaults.
findings.suggestion: Extract tag ID constants from factory defaults and reference them in test assertions.

findings.summary: TaxonomyCache._get_or_create_item_id accepts a kind_factory callable for two trivial cases, adding unnecessary indirection.
findings.suggestion: Pass the _TaxonomyKind directly instead of wrapping it in a zero-argument factory callable.

findings.summary: The Settings class in common.config contains both OCR-specific and classifier-specific fields, coupling both domain packages to a single shared configuration object.
evidence: self.OCR_DPI alongside self.CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID in the same Settings class
findings.suggestion: Consider composing per-package config sections (e.g. OcrSettings, ClassifierSettings) rather than one flat class.

findings.summary: One call site in _requeue_for_ocr wraps tags in list() before passing to update_document_metadata, while all other callers pass sets directly.
findings.suggestion: Remove the list() wrapping to be consistent, since update_document_metadata handles conversion internally.

findings.summary: Both httpx and the openai SDK (which bundles httpx internally) are declared as dependencies, meaning two HTTP client stacks are in play.
evidence: httpx~=0.28 and openai~=1.35 both in dependencies
findings.suggestion: This is acceptable since they serve different APIs, but verify httpx is not duplicating functionality available through the openai SDK.

findings.summary: Test files had excessive single-test class proliferation typical of LLM-generated code, now consolidated but some single-test classes remain.
evidence: TestTagEnrichment contains only one test method
findings.suggestion: Consider merging remaining single-test classes into their closest thematic neighbor.

findings.summary: OCR-specific REFUSAL_PHRASES are imported from common.constants into the shared Settings class, embedding OCR domain knowledge in the shared layer.
evidence: from .constants import REFUSAL_PHRASES used in Settings.__init__ for OCR_REFUSAL_MARKERS default
findings.suggestion: Move REFUSAL_PHRASES to the ocr package or make it a plain default value to keep the shared config layer domain-agnostic.

findings.summary: Settings class mixes shared infrastructure fields with domain-specific OCR and classifier configuration, reducing clarity of ownership.
evidence: OCR_DPI, OCR_MAX_SIDE, PAGE_WORKERS alongside CLASSIFY_PERSON_FIELD_ID, CLASSIFY_MAX_CHARS in the same class
findings.suggestion: Group domain-specific fields into nested config objects or separate dataclasses composed by the main Settings.

findings.summary: Tags cross module boundaries as both set[int] and list[int] inconsistently: internal code uses sets, but API layer expects lists.
evidence: update_document_metadata internally converts value = list(value) for tags
findings.suggestion: Standardize on one type at module boundaries and document the convention.
